---
title: "Mitochondrial length"
date: "January 11th, 2026"
author: "Tyler Weide"
format:
  html:
    theme: "Cosmo"
    toc: true
    number-depth: 3
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    page-layout: full
    embed-resources: true
    standalone: true
---

## 
<!-- Set Knitr Options -->

```{r}
#| label: knitr_setup
#| warning: false
#| echo: false

# set options for knitr (engine that will render everything)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo       = TRUE,
                      warning    = FALSE,
                      error      = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      fig.height = 6,
                      fig.width  = 8
                      )

```

<!-- Setup Code -->
# Setting up the code and reading in the dataset
```{r}

#| label: setup_code 
#| warning: FALSE
#| echo: TRUE

#==============================================================================#
# Setup Options
#==============================================================================#

# remove all objects if restarting script
rm(list=ls())

# set options for R
options(
  tibble.width = Inf,   # print all columns in tibble
  scipen = 999          # remove scientific notation
)

suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidyverse))
#==============================================================================#
# Packages
#==============================================================================#

# load libraries
library(knitr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpattern)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(effectsize)
#==============================================================================#

#==============================================================================#

# set all paths
path_main    <- "."
#path_data    <- str_c(path_main, "Data/",    sep="")
#path_plots   <- str_c(path_main, "Plots",   sep="")
#path_scripts <- str_c(path_main, "Scripts/", sep="")

#==============================================================================#
# Set Inputs
#==============================================================================#

# File names
LowMito <- "Low_measurements.csv"
HighMito <- "High_measurements.csv"


#==============================================================================#
# Check session info again
#==============================================================================#

# check sessionInfo again
#sessionInfo()


#Read in the combined dataset

Low <- read_delim(
  file      = LowMito,
  skip = 1,
  delim     = ",",
  col_names = c("Number", "Measurement"),
  col_types = "nn")

High <- read_delim(
  file      = HighMito,
  skip = 1,
  delim     = ",",
  col_names = c("Number", "Measurement"),
  col_types = "nn")
```

# Welch's T-test
```{r}
#| label: Welch's T-test
#| warning: false
#| echo: false

# Welch's Two-sided T-test (default, does NOT assume equal variance here)
t.test(High$Measurement, Low$Measurement)

# This shows effect size, not pooling SD
cohens_d(
  High$Measurement,
  Low$Measurement,
  pooled_sd = FALSE)

```

# Plot for publication
```{r}
#| label: Plots
#| warning: false
#| echo: false

# Create dataframe that binds the rows of both low and high, order them as such
mito_df <- bind_rows(
  High %>% mutate(Population = "High"),
  Low  %>% mutate(Population = "Low")) %>%
  mutate(Population = factor(Population, levels = c("Low", "High")))

# plot
Raincloud_Plot <- ggplot(mito_df, aes(x = Population, y = Measurement)) +
  geom_violin(trim = FALSE, fill = "grey90", color = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.18, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.18, size = 0.55) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 2.2) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_cl_normal, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.08) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  xlab("Population") +
  ylab("Mitochondrial sheath length (µm)")

Raincloud_Plot

# save at or above 300dpi as PNG
ggsave(filename = "Mitochondrial_Sheath_Length.png", plot = Raincloud_Plot, width = 14, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```
